title: Meta and Intuit cut 11,000 jobs in tech's biggest layoff day of 2026
subtitle: The two companies shed staff the same morning — but told opposite stories about whether AI was the reason.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: On May 20, Meta and Intuit eliminated roughly 11,000 jobs between them, the single biggest day of tech layoffs this year. Meta began notifying about 8,000 employees — close to 10% of its workforce and its third cut of 2026 — starting at 4 a.m. local time. Intuit, the maker of TurboTax and QuickBooks, cut about 3,000 people, or 17% of its global staff, the same morning. By the end of the week, more than 114,000 tech workers had been laid off in 2026.

The two companies told opposite stories about why. Meta openly framed its cuts as an AI pivot, moving roughly 7,000 remaining workers into new units called Applied AI Engineering and the Agent Transformation Accelerator, tasked with building agents to handle coding, research, analytics, and internal operations. Intuit CEO Sasan Goodarzi rejected that framing outright, telling CNBC the layoffs "had nothing to do with AI" and were about streamlining operations and improving execution.

That split captures a debate running through 2026. "AI efficiency" has become the default explanation for headcount reductions — sometimes accurately, sometimes as cover for ordinary cost-cutting or a correction to pandemic-era over-hiring. The tension is not hypothetical: days earlier, Standard Chartered's chief executive publicly apologized for having blamed job cuts on AI. Whether AI is the cause or the cover, the headcount math keeps moving in the same direction.

Takeaway for learners: watch what companies do with the people they keep, not just the ones they cut. Meta is not shrinking its AI ambitions — it is concentrating talent into agent-building teams, which tells you where the durable roles are. For an early-career engineer, the signal is to get genuinely fluent at building and supervising AI agents. And read every "we cut jobs because of AI" headline with a skeptical eye — as Intuit's own CEO demonstrated, sometimes that explanation is true, and sometimes it is a tidy narrative laid over a messier balance sheet.

sources.title: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs as it pivots towards AI — NPR
Intuit CEO says company's 17% workforce cut had 'nothing to do with AI' — CNBC
